title: Departure
summary: Leaving home. The Haines brothers scatter from airports across the country, all converging on Paris.

title: Arrival & Travel to Normandy
summary: Five brothers reunite at Charles de Gaulle. Rental car secured, we drive three hours west to Bayeux — the staging ground for tomorrow's pilgrimage to the beaches.
lodging: Hôtel Le Bayeux, 9 Rue Tardif, 14400 Bayeux, France

stops.name: Charles de Gaulle Airport
stops.note: Doug, Nathan & Talmage arrive first. Josh at 11:30, Cam last at 12:40.

stops.name: Bayeux
stops.note: Arrive at the hotel. Groceries, dinner, early to bed.

title: Normandy — D-Day Beaches
summary: A full day walking the ground where the liberation of Europe began. West to east across the beaches, cemeteries, and bluffs of the Normandy coast.
lodging: Hôtel Le Bayeux, 9 Rue Tardif, 14400 Bayeux, France

stops.name: Omaha Beach
stops.note: Where the heaviest losses occurred on June 6, 1944.

stops.name: Normandy American Cemetery
stops.note: 9,388 American graves overlooking Omaha Beach.

stops.name: Pointe du Hoc
stops.note: Ranger assault point — craters still visible.

title: Normandy Morning & Drive to Aachen
summary: Last morning on the Normandy coast, catching sites we missed. Then a five-hour drive east across France, Belgium, and into Germany. Grandpa's war is about to become real.

stops.name: Bayeux (departure)
stops.note: Final Normandy stops, then eastbound.

stops.name: Aachen
stops.note: Arrive at the Airbnb. Dinner and grocery run.

title: Grandpa's WWII Sites
summary: The heart of the trip. Walking the ground Grandpa walked — the brick factory at Huchlen, Frenzerburg Castle, the forests around Schevenhütte where he first saw combat, and Höfen where he was wounded. Albert Trosdorf, a local historian, guides us through the morning.

stops.name: Langerwehe-Merode (Albert Trosdorf)
stops.note: Pick up Albert at Schlossstrasse 12. Tour of the brick factory and Frenzerburg Castle.

stops.name: Frenzerburg Castle
stops.note: Where Grandpa's unit was positioned.

stops.name: Hürtgen Forest
stops.note: The forest where some of the war's bloodiest fighting occurred.

stops.name: Schevenhütte
stops.note: Where Grandpa first arrived on the front lines.

stops.name: Vossenack / Schmidt
stops.note: Key Hürtgen Forest battle sites.

stops.name: Höfen area
stops.note: Near where Grandpa was wounded.

stops.name: Aachen Cathedral
stops.note: Evening in Aachen before heading back.

title: Aachen Morning & Travel to Paris
summary: A quieter morning — last looks at the Hürtgen area or exploring Aachen. Dad and Uncle Doug split off by train. The rest of us drive the van back toward Paris.
lodging: Airbnb, 23 Rue de Nantes, Mitry-Mory, Île-de-France 77290

stops.name: Aachen (departure)
stops.note: Check out. Dad and Doug take trains. Brothers drive to Paris.

stops.name: Mitry-Mory (north of Paris)
stops.note: Airbnb near CDG for the night.

title: Paris
summary: Our last full day together. The brothers take Paris — the Eiffel Tower, the Seine, the streets. A different kind of history, a lighter step. After five days of walking hallowed ground, we walk the City of Light.
lodging: Airbnb, 23 Rue de Nantes, Mitry-Mory, Île-de-France 77290

title: Paris with Kara
summary: A full day in Paris with Kara — morning Mass at Notre-Dame, the Arc de Triomphe at noon, the Catacombs in the afternoon, dinner at JJ Beaumarchais, and the Eiffel Tower shimmering over Trocadéro at night.
lodging: Airbnb, 23 Rue de Nantes, Mitry-Mory, Île-de-France 77290

stops.name: Boulevard Beaumarchais / Marais
stops.note: Morning coffee and walk through the Marais.

stops.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
stops.note: Morning Mass at Notre-Dame.

stops.name: Arc de Triomphe
stops.note: Tomb of the Unknown Soldier and the view down the Champs-Élysées.

stops.name: Catacombs
stops.note: Descending into the ossuaries beneath Paris.

stops.name: JJ Beaumarchais
stops.note: Dinner at 92 Blvd Beaumarchais.

stops.name: Trocadéro / Eiffel Tower
stops.note: Eiffel Tower at night from Trocadéro.

title: Departure
summary: Cam and Kara leave Paris for home — 7:30 AM out the door, Aer Lingus via Dublin, and back to Minneapolis.

stops.name: Charles de Gaulle Airport
stops.note: EI 521 CDG → Dublin at 10:20 AM, then EI 89 Dublin → MSP.
